notes: Hand-updated operational policy aligned with canonical knowledge docs and exact discovered route names.
Read routes are preferred over action routes.
Protected audio paths and trigger-style controls must preserve service-layer logic.
All mapped actions must still be validated against route and tool allowlists.

special_rules.name: living_room_audio_protection
special_rules.rule: Preserve protected living-room prepare/play/release behavior through existing services. Do not bypass service-layer handling.

special_rules.name: trigger_style_controls
special_rules.rule: Treat these as approved trigger-style controls. Do not assume persistent device state beyond documented behavior.

special_rules.name: parameterized_node_power_route
special_rules.rule: Use only approved state values such as on or off and preserve room-mapping and service-layer control logic.

special_rules.name: no_recursive_agent_execution
special_rules.rule: The autonomous agent must not call its own top-level natural-language route as an action target.

rules: Use /ai/audio_timing_health for quick speaker timing health checks.
Use /ai/audio_timing_last for detailed diagnostics and debugging context.
Use /ai/audio_output_confidence when the user asks whether playback is likely audible or trustworthy.
Use /ai/audio_timing_run only when the user explicitly asks to validate or rerun speaker timing.
Do not run audible timing tests automatically without explicit user intent.
